title: Git node argument injection via unseparated user paths passed to git.add/git.commit
description: The Git node passes user-controlled parameters directly into git command arguments. In the 'add' operation the pathsToAdd parameter is split on commas and handed to git.add() without an '--' end-of-options separator, and the 'commit' operation does the same for committed paths. Because the values precede '--', an attacker (an authenticated user who can create or modify workflows) can supply values that begin with '-' so git interprets them as options rather than path operands, achieving argument injection. Combined with a separate bug where validateGitReference validated the constant 'HEAD' instead of the user-supplied options.reference, this allowed dangerous git options (for example option-style references such as '-n', or git arguments that point to external files/commands) to be smuggled into git invocations, leading to arbitrary file read and OS command execution on the n8n host.
